records.ety: [F. rsurrection, L. resurrectio, fr. resurgere, resurrectum, to rise again; pref. re- re- + surgere to rise. See Source.]

defs: A rising again; the resumption of vigor.
Especially, the rising again from the dead; the resumption of life by the dead; as, the resurrection of Jesus Christ; the general resurrection of all the dead at the Day of Judgment.
quotes: Nor after resurrection shall he stay Longer on earth.

defs: State of being risen from the dead; future state.
quotes: In the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage.

defs: The cause or exemplar of a rising from the dead.
quotes: I am the resurrection, and the life.
